title: Tristemma theifolium

The classification of Tristemma theifolium is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Myrtales, Family: Melastomataceae.

intro: Tristemma theifolium, also known as the "Wild Jasmine", is a plant species that belongs to the family Melastomataceae, characterized by its small, white flowers and leaves with a distinctive fragrance. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Tristemma theifolium include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties.
